project.name: steampunk-strategy
project.description: Central financial management, compliance tracking, and cross-site operations dashboard for Steampunk Farms Rescue Barn Inc. Manages 41 Prisma models across financial, compliance, fundraising, and operational domains. Serves as the SFOS integration hub with cross-site data flows to Studiolo, Rescue Barn, and Cleanpunk.

nodes.name: check-vercel-env.js
nodes.summary: Pre-deploy validation script that verifies all required environment variables are set in the Vercel project, preventing deployments with missing configuration.

nodes.name: deploy-orchestrator.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script automating orchestrator deployment: validates environment, runs type checks, verifies cron registry consistency, commits, pushes, and monitors the Vercel deployment.

nodes.name: eip-phase1-setup.ts
nodes.summary: Email Intelligence Pipeline Phase 1 setup script that creates initial database records, configures Gmail query patterns, and establishes vendor-email mappings for automated email processing.

nodes.name: fb-types.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive TypeScript type definitions for Facebook data export structures (messages, posts, reactions, comments, followers, fundraisers) and their normalized counterparts for database import.

nodes.name: name-parser.ts
nodes.summary: Parses Facebook post titles and user names to extract individual names, interaction types (liked, commented, shared), and split full names into first/last components for donor matching.

nodes.name: extractNameFromTitle
nodes.summary: Extracts a person's name from a Facebook post title by removing action verbs and page references.

nodes.name: splitName
nodes.summary: Splits a full name string into first and last name components.

nodes.name: utf8-fix.ts
nodes.summary: Fixes Facebook's broken UTF-8 encoding in data exports where non-ASCII characters are stored as mojibake Latin-1 byte sequences, restoring proper Unicode text.

nodes.name: fixFacebookString
nodes.summary: Converts a single Facebook mojibake string back to proper UTF-8 by re-encoding Latin-1 bytes.

nodes.name: fixFacebookEncoding
nodes.summary: Recursively walks a JSON object and fixes all Facebook mojibake strings in-place.

nodes.name: phase0-normalize.ts
nodes.summary: Phase 0 of the Facebook data import pipeline that normalizes raw Facebook export data by fixing UTF-8 encoding issues and parsing donor names from post titles.

nodes.name: import-amazon-mbox.ts
nodes.summary: Parses Amazon order confirmation emails from an mbox file, extracts order details and amounts, categorizes purchases, and imports them as expense transactions into Prisma.

nodes.name: import-amazon-ordered-mbox.ts
nodes.summary: Parses Amazon 'order placed' confirmation emails from mbox format, extracting order amounts and categorizing purchases for import into the expense tracking database.

nodes.name: import-elstons-2025.ts
nodes.summary: Imports 2025 Elstons feed store receipts and expense records into the Prisma database for livestock feed cost tracking.

nodes.name: import-godaddy-mbox.ts
nodes.summary: Parses GoDaddy receipt emails from mbox files, extracts domain renewal and hosting line items with amounts, and imports them as expense transactions into Prisma.

nodes.name: import-meta-payouts.ts
nodes.summary: Imports Meta/Facebook fundraiser payout records into the Prisma database, tracking donation revenue received through Meta's charitable giving platform.

nodes.name: import-other-receipts-mbox.ts
nodes.summary: Parses miscellaneous vendor receipt emails from mbox files, classifying them by email sender/kind, extracting transaction amounts, and importing as expense records into Prisma.

nodes.name: ingest-vet-files.ts
nodes.summary: Ingests veterinary record PDF files by uploading them to Vercel Blob storage, using Claude AI to extract structured medical data, and saving parsed vet records to Prisma.

nodes.name: parse-mbox-expenses.ts
nodes.summary: General-purpose mbox email parser that extracts expense-related emails, parses transaction details from email bodies, and imports them as expense records into Prisma.

nodes.name: position-bias-analysis.mjs
nodes.summary: Analyzes position bias in AI evaluation results by reading JSON test data and computing statistical metrics to detect whether item ordering affects scoring outcomes.

nodes.name: protocol-health-summary.mjs
nodes.summary: Generates a summary health report of protocol compliance across the codebase by reading JSON scan results and computing aggregate statistics like sums and averages.

nodes.name: repromote-unmapped.ts
nodes.summary: Re-runs the cost promotion logic on unmapped expense line items, using the cost-tracker-promoter mappings to categorize previously uncategorized transactions.

nodes.name: roadmap-updater.js
nodes.summary: CLI utility that reads and updates the project roadmap markdown file, performing automated edits to task status and completion markers.

nodes.name: scan-pledge-collection.ts
nodes.summary: Scans Gmail via the Google API for pledge-related donation emails, extracts donor and amount information from message headers and bodies for pledge collection tracking.

nodes.name: seed-credential-registry.ts
nodes.summary: Seeds the Prisma database with a comprehensive registry of credentials, API keys, and service accounts used across all web properties, tracking expiration dates and renewal requirements.

nodes.name: seed-meta-vendor.ts
nodes.summary: Seeds the Prisma database with a Meta/Facebook vendor record for use in expense and revenue tracking of Meta platform transactions.

nodes.name: seed-saas-subscriptions.ts
nodes.summary: Seeds the Prisma database with SaaS subscription records (Vercel, GitHub, domain registrars, etc.) including billing cycles, amounts, and renewal dates for cost tracking.

nodes.name: sync-gofundme-to-studiolo.ts
nodes.summary: Scans Gmail for GoFundMe donation notification emails via Google API, parses donor names and gift amounts, and syncs them into the Studiolo CRM database as gift records.

nodes.name: validate-env.sh
nodes.summary: Bash script that validates required environment variables are set before deployment, checking for database URLs, API keys, and auth secrets.

nodes.name: verify-handoff.mjs
nodes.summary: Comprehensive handoff verification script that scans the codebase for protocol compliance, checking file structure, naming conventions, and required documentation artifacts against governance rules.

nodes.name: tailwind.config.js
nodes.summary: Tailwind CSS configuration defining custom theme colors, fonts, spacing, and animation utilities with the tailwindcss-animate plugin for the TARDIS dashboard UI.

nodes.name: verify-handoff.mjs
nodes.summary: Root-level re-export wrapper that delegates to the actual handoff verification implementation in scripts/verify-handoff.mjs for convenient CLI invocation.

nodes.name: write
nodes.summary: Writes normalized data to a JSON output file for the next phase of the Facebook import pipeline.

nodes.name: scanDir
nodes.summary: Recursively scans a directory tree collecting file paths for handoff protocol compliance checking.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Route handlers under app/api/ implementing 89+ endpoints across financial management, compliance, intelligence, revenue, document processing, and cron-triggered jobs. Primary consumer of the Service Layer (154 imports).

layers.name: Page Layer
layers.description: Protected and public page components under app/(protected)/ and app/(public)/. Covers board minutes, compliance dashboards, cost centers, expense tracking, gift staging, intelligence views, monitoring, tax hub, transparency reports, and the login page.

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Core business logic modules in lib/: Prisma client, NextAuth configuration, alerting, Anthropic Claude AI integration, intelligence analysis engine, cost allocation, document parsing, transparency calculations, and cryptographic utilities. Highest fan-in (186 inbound imports) with zero outbound dependencies to other layers.

layers.name: UI Components
layers.description: Shared React components in components/: chart visualizations, status badges, sidebar navigation, keyboard shortcut handlers, and UI primitives. Consumed by the Page Layer (8 imports) with moderate internal cohesion.

layers.name: Tooling Layer
layers.description: CLI utilities and migration scripts in scripts/: database seeding, audit trails, data migrations, mbox email parsing, and one-off administrative operations. Largely standalone with minimal cross-layer dependencies.

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Root configuration and framework plumbing: Next.js config, middleware (auth guards), instrumentation, ESLint/Tailwind/PostCSS configs, Prisma schema, app layout, TypeScript type definitions, and documentation assets.

tour.title: The Bridge — Command Center
tour.description: The Bridge is TARDIS's main dashboard, serving as the central operations hub for Steampunk Farms Rescue Barn Inc. It aggregates financial KPIs, compliance status, and cross-site health into a single protected view. Everything in the application radiates outward from this entry point.

tour.title: Auth & Middleware — Azure AD Gate
tour.description: All protected routes require Azure AD authentication via NextAuth. The auth configuration, session provider, and middleware enforce that only authorized board members and staff can access financial and compliance data. The login page is the only public entry point.

tour.title: Financial Core — Transactions & Expenses
tour.description: The Prisma client is the data backbone, connecting 41 models covering transactions, expenses, vendors, and cost centers. Transaction and expense API routes handle CRUD operations, while the expenses UI provides search, filtering, analytics, and bulk operations for day-to-day financial management.

tour.title: Document Pipeline — Upload to Transaction
tour.description: Documents (receipts, invoices, checks) are uploaded, then parsed by Claude AI to extract structured financial data. The receipt-parser and invoice-pipeline modules use the Anthropic SDK to read scanned documents, extract line items, and automatically create matching transactions in the database.

tour.title: Vendor Management — Suppliers & Arrangements
tour.description: The vendor module maintains a directory of all suppliers and service providers. Vendor matching logic auto-links incoming documents to known vendors, while the arrangements system tracks recurring contracts, payment terms, and service agreements.

tour.title: Cost Allocation Engine
tour.description: The cost allocation engine distributes expenses across programs and cost centers, essential for nonprofit fund accounting. The allocation-engine library computes splits, the cost-tracker promoter surfaces unallocated items, and the cost-centers UI provides visibility into where money flows across the organization.

tour.title: Compliance Tracking — Filings & Deadlines
tour.description: As a 501(c)(3), compliance is critical. The compliance module tracks filing deadlines, regulatory tasks, and organizational requirements. The compliance scanner automatically detects upcoming obligations, while the tax hub aggregates tax-related data for annual filings.

tour.title: Board Minutes — Meetings to PDF
tour.description: The board minutes module captures the full lifecycle of board meetings: a multi-step wizard collects metadata, attendees, and agenda items, then uses Claude AI to polish raw notes into formal minutes. The system generates PDFs and captures attestation signatures for legal compliance.

tour.title: Intelligence Suite — AI-Powered Insights
tour.description: The intelligence module provides analytical dashboards, board packs, forecasting, and scenario modeling. The AI insights engine uses Claude to generate strategic recommendations, while expense aggregations and analytical queries surface trends. The scenario engine lets leadership model financial what-ifs.

tour.title: Cross-Site Integrations
tour.description: TARDIS connects to sibling sites across the Steampunk ecosystem. Gift staging pushes donor data to Studiolo (CRM), the transparency API feeds public financial data to Rescue Barn's website, and the product-species map syncs inventory categories with Cleanpunk (e-commerce). Cross-site contracts define shared type interfaces.

tour.title: Revenue Tracking — Retail Charity & Dev Costs
tour.description: The retail charity module tracks RaiseRight gift card revenue, a key fundraising channel for the nonprofit. The upload form ingests earnings reports, charts visualize trends, and automated reminders keep the program active. The dev-costs section monitors technology spending with projection charts.

tour.title: Alerting & Notifications
tour.description: The alerting system sends real-time notifications via Resend (email) and Twilio (SMS) for compliance deadlines, financial anomalies, and system events. The monitoring module and alert feed component provide a live dashboard of recent alerts and system health status.

tour.title: Scripts & Tooling — Migrations and Audits
tour.description: A rich collection of CLI scripts handles data migrations (mbox email parsing, Amazon imports, GoFundMe syncs), audit trails, compliance backfills, and database seeding. These tools support the operational lifecycle beyond the web UI, enabling bulk data operations and integrity checks.
